input.description:
**Files:**
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/get.rs` (line 42)
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/delete.rs` (line 46)
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/update.rs` (line 80)

**What:** All three files use `.map_err(|_| KanbanError::NotFound { ... })` when reading the attachment entity. The `|_|` closure discards the original error. If the entity read fails for a reason other than "not found" (e.g., YAML parse error, I/O permission error), the caller gets a misleading `NotFound` error, making debugging difficult.

**Why it matters:** The entity layer distinguishes `EntityError::NotFound` from `EntityError::Io` and `EntityError::Yaml`. Mapping all failures to `NotFound` hides legitimate I/O or parse errors. This violates the principle that error messages should help diagnose the actual problem.

**Suggestion:**
- [ ] Match on the error variant: convert `EntityError::NotFound` to `KanbanError::NotFound`, but let other errors propagate with their original context (e.g., via `KanbanError::from(entity_error)` or a dedicated variant)
- [ ] Apply this pattern consistently across `get.rs`, `delete.rs`, and `update.rs`
- [ ] Verify by running tests: `cargo nextest run --package swissarmyhammer-kanban`
input.title: map_err discards original entity error context in get/delete/update

-**Files:**
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/get.rs` (line 42)
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/delete.rs` (line 46)
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/update.rs` (line 80)

\ No newline at end of file
+**Done.** Replaced error-discarding `map_err(|_| ...)` closures in attachment get/update with `map_err(KanbanError::from_entity_error)`. Updated from_entity_error catch-all to map unknown entity types to `NotFound { resource, id }` instead of generic EntityError wrapper.

- [x] Replace closures in attachment get.rs, update.rs
- [x] from_entity_error now handles all entity types including attachment
- [x] 216 tests pass, clippy clean
\ No newline at end of file
